OLYMPIA, Feb. 9.-The cover
nor has just appointed a commits
sion consisting OF Charles p. Lund
of Spokane, l.. o. Meigs of North
Yakima, speaker of the house of
the last legislatures; Carroll b.
Graves, attorney of Seattle; j. D.
Blackwell, civil engineer of Seat
tie, and Prof c. l.. Waller, dean OF
the faculty of the state college at
Pullman. The purpose Of the COM
mission is set forth in the follow
ing letters:


Realizing the necessity for bet
ter laws for system of state con-
troll Of our water courses, water
ways and water powers, l inclose
you herewith commission ap-
pointing you to take up this work
for the public good, that you may
gather data and information and
have bill prepared ready to sub
NIT to the twelfth session of the
legislature. You are doubtless
aware that there is no authorize
ton of law or appropriation made
to cover the expenses of this COM
mission, and the work must be en-
tirely voluntary on your part"
